steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all angles in any triangle is 180°. Given angle X = 108° and angle Y = 36°, we write: $$X + Y + Z = 180°$$ Substituting the known values: $$108° + 36° + Z = 180°$$

steps.title: Solve for angle Z
steps.content: Subtract the known angles from both sides: $$Z = 180° - 108° - 36° = 36°$$ Angles Y and Z are both 36°, confirming the problem's condition that two angles are equal. That matches choice A — A is correct.
